commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this protected draft adds a new core snapshot/artifact contract and the remaining proof gate requires contributor or maintainer evidence rather than an automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the final 7-file diff; the earlier lockfile concern no longer applies. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a private core SQLite snapshot provider and manifest format, exports a shared VACUUM INTO helper from memory-host-sdk, tests the provider, and reuses the helper in backup creation.

PR surface: Source +388, Tests +242. Total +630 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR adds a new snapshot provider capability. Source review confirms the provider and WAL-mode tests exist, but this read-only review did not run tests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this protected draft adds a new core snapshot/artifact contract and the remaining proof gate requires contributor or maintainer evidence rather than an automated repair.

target.title: Add snapshot provider proof plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the bundled `snapshot` plugin scaffold under `extensions/snapshot` without exposing a public CLI or public provider barrel yet.
- Proves the first step of the RFC's safe-sync-file story: turn a live SQLite database into a verified artifact + manifest that a file-syncing host can persist.
- Adds a private local `SqliteSnapshotProvider` backed by a manifest + compact `database.sqlite` artifact repository.
- Builds on #94646's database-first layout: global/control-plane SQLite and per-agent/data-plane SQLite are the long-term targets; this PR proves the mechanism against one explicit DB reference.
- Shares the SQLite `VACUUM INTO` primitive with the existing backup path through `@openclaw/memory-host-sdk`, including sqlite-vec loading and compact-snapshot behavior.
- Proves committed WAL-mode state is captured without asking hosts to sync live `*.sqlite-wal`, `*.sqlite-shm`, or `*.sqlite-journal` sidecars.
- Keeps the lockfile scoped to workspace links only; the draft plugin no longer pulls a published `openclaw` package or changes existing `undici` resolution.

## Why this matters

Scout/Lobster-style hosts persist OpenClaw by syncing files when they are saved. A live SQLite directory is the wrong durability boundary: the main `*.sqlite` file can be incomplete without WAL state, and unmanaged file deltas over live SQLite can observe DB pages, WAL frames, and sidecars at different moments.

This PR proves the safer boundary: SQLite materializes a clean artifact first, the provider verifies it, and the host can sync the completed artifact set instead of the live DB working set. Deltas remain a future optimization after verified snapshot artifacts and cursors exist.

## Scope

This is PR 1 from the snapshot RFC roadmap: provider proof only. It intentionally does not add `openclaw snapshot ...` commands, backup command integration, remote storage, leases, scheduling, or failover orchestration.

RFC context: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/20
Database-first state layout: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94646
Related storage discussion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90370

## Verification

- `pnpm install --ignore-scripts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts` - passed, 1 file / 6 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/sqlite-vec.test.ts` - passed, 1 file / 9 tests
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint packages\memory-host-sdk\src\host\sqlite-snapshot.ts packages\memory-host-sdk\src\engine-storage.ts extensions\snapshot\src\local-repository.ts src\infra\backup-create.ts` - passed
- `node --import tsx -e "await import('./extensions/snapshot/src/local-repository.ts'); await import('./packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/sqlite-snapshot.ts'); await import('./src/infra/backup-create.ts'); console.log('imports ok')"` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- Previous baseline check: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/backup-create.test.ts` was 37/38 on Windows; the remaining `EBUSY` cleanup failure reproduced unchanged on a clean `origin/main` worktree, so it is treated as base noise rather than this patch.
- Previous typecheck check: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/snapshot/tsconfig.json --noEmit` was blocked by unrelated current worktree/package-resolution errors in `packages/gateway-client`, `@openclaw/net-policy`, and `dist/plugin-sdk` duplicate `EventStream` declarations before any snapshot-specific error.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: SQLite snapshot provider can create, verify, list, and internally restore compact snapshot artifacts from SQLite databases, including WAL-mode state. This proves the safe sync artifact boundary for the database-first SQLite target model landed in #94646.
Real environment tested: Windows source checkout, Node/Vitest extension test runner against `node:sqlite`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Test suite creates a WAL-mode source database, commits a row after `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, snapshots via the local provider, verifies the manifest and artifact, restores to a fresh path, and reads both pre-WAL and committed-in-WAL rows from the restored database. It also covers failed create cleanup, artifact tamper detection, signed `user_version`, same-millisecond snapshot IDs, and stale restore-target WAL sidecar removal.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed; 6 provider tests passed.
What was not tested: Public `openclaw snapshot` CLI, backup command integration, object storage providers, scheduled snapshots, leases, and fresh OpenClaw state-dir boot proof; those are intentionally later PRs.
